etymology.description: 𫠤 (depicting a person with a headdress) -> person stylized as 夭 (young man walking) + 攴 (authority) added -> transformed into 𢾍 -> further stylized to incorporate 方
etymology.interpretation: The character 敖(敖) (áo, "proud") evolved from a pictogram depicting a person with a headdress, which over time incorporated elements suggesting movement and authority. The final form integrates these elements to convey a sense of pride or authority in one's bearing.
